metadata.episodesArchiveDescription: Browse every Decoder episode — filter by language and topic, search the archive, and play in full.

about.label: About the Show
about.p1: Decoder is a podcast for people who build things — and for those curious about what goes into the games and tech they love. We go beyond the surface, beyond the press releases, and into the actual craft.
about.p2: Each episode is an honest conversation between a game designer and a technologist — two different lenses on the same evolving landscape. We argue, we agree, and we always learn something.
about.p3: No fluff. No filler. Just ideas worth your time.

jan.bio: Game designer with a decade of experience crafting interactive systems. Jan has shipped titles across mobile, PC, and console — and never stopped asking why players do what they do. His obsession: the invisible rules that make games feel alive.
jan.specialtyTopics: Systems design, player psychology, game feel

martin.bio: Founder and technical architect who builds the infrastructure games run on. Martin has gone from writing assembly code to leading product teams — and finds the intersection of engineering and creativity the most interesting place to be.
